edges.description: Reduced HDAC4 dosage disrupts skeletal growth and distal phalangeal development.
Contiguous gene loss perturbs brain development and behavior.
Deletion of 2q37 genes contributes to short stature and obesity.
Distal skeletal patterning defects produce brachydactyly type E.
Altered growth regulation contributes to short stature.
Developmental neurobiologic disruption presents as global delay.
Persistent neurodevelopmental disruption causes intellectual disability.
Brain developmental differences contribute to autism-spectrum and other behavioral features.
Central hypotonia contributes to infantile motor delay.
Altered growth and metabolic regulation manifests as obesity or overweight.
Haploinsufficiency of a tumor-suppressor gene in the 2q37.1 interval may contribute to Wilms tumor predisposition in larger deletions.

nodes.description: Terminal or interstitial deletions of chromosome 2q37 create a contiguous gene haploinsufficiency syndrome, with HDAC4 emerging as the major driver of the core brachydactyly-intellectual disability phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: 2q37 Microdeletion Syndrome – RETIRED CHAPTER, FOR HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY.
evidence.snippet: Mutation of HDAC4 has been proposed as causative for most of the features of the 2q37 microdeletion syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: A family with brachydactyly mental retardation syndrome with a missense variant in HDAC4.
evidence.snippet: Brachydactyly mental retardation syndrome (BDMR) or chromosome 2q37 deletion syndrome is a genetic disorder caused by 2q37 deletion or haploinsufficiency of histone deacetylase 4 (HDAC4).

nodes.description: Behavioral features are a recurring part of the syndrome and may include attention deficit and hyperactivity.

evidence.reference_title: The 2q37-deletion syndrome: an update of the clinical spectrum including overweight, brachydactyly and behavioural features in 14 new patients.
evidence.snippet: Patients exhibited facial dysmorphism (13/14) and brachydactyly (10/14), associated with behavioural problems, autism or autism spectrum disorders of varying severity and overweight or obesity.

nodes.description: Brachydactyly type E / brachymetaphalangy is one of the most characteristic skeletal findings.

evidence.reference_title: The 2q37-deletion syndrome: an update of the clinical spectrum including overweight, brachydactyly and behavioural features in 14 new patients.
evidence.snippet: Patients exhibited facial dysmorphism (13/14) and brachydactyly (10/14), associated with behavioural problems, autism or autism spectrum disorders of varying severity and overweight or obesity.

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-Phenotype Correlations in 2q37-Deletion Syndrome: An Update of the Clinical Spectrum and Literature Review
evidence.snippet: 2q37 microdeletion/deletion syndrome (2q37DS) ... is characterized by a broad and diverse spectrum of clinical findings: characteristic facial dysmorphism, developmental delay/intellectual disability (ID), brachydactyly type E ... 8/9 skeletal anomalies—especially brachydactyly type E

nodes.description: Larger 2q37 deletions extend beyond HDAC4 to encompass additional dosage-sensitive genes, producing a more severe and variable phenotype. Deletions including 2q37.1 carry a risk of Wilms tumor through haploinsufficiency of genes in that interval. Structural chromosome rearrangements (inversions, ring chromosomes, translocations involving 2q37) can produce the same contiguous gene loss at variable breakpoints.

evidence.reference_title: 2q37 Microdeletion Syndrome – RETIRED CHAPTER, FOR HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY.
evidence.snippet: In some individuals, 2q37 microdeletion syndrome results from chromosome rearrangements involving 2q37 (e.g., chromosome 2 inversion, ring chromosome 2, or translocation between chromosome 2 and another chromosome).

evidence.reference_title: 2q37 Microdeletion Syndrome – RETIRED CHAPTER, FOR HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY.
evidence.snippet: For young children with a deletion that includes 2q37.1, screening for Wilms tumor can be considered.

nodes.description: Developmental delay or intellectual disability is the dominant presentation.

evidence.reference_title: 2q37 Microdeletion Syndrome – RETIRED CHAPTER, FOR HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY.
evidence.snippet: CLINICAL CHARACTERISTICS: 2q37 microdeletion syndrome is characterized by mild-moderate developmental delay/intellectual disability, brachymetaphalangy of digits 3-5 (often digit 4 alone) (>50%), short stature, obesity, hypotonia, characteristic facial appearance, autism or autism spectrum disorder (30%), joint hypermobility/dislocation, and scoliosis.

nodes.description: Infantile hypotonia is part of the classic phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: 2q37 Microdeletion Syndrome – RETIRED CHAPTER, FOR HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY.
evidence.snippet: CLINICAL CHARACTERISTICS: 2q37 microdeletion syndrome is characterized by mild-moderate developmental delay/intellectual disability, brachymetaphalangy of digits 3-5 (often digit 4 alone) (>50%), short stature, obesity, hypotonia, characteristic facial appearance, autism or autism spectrum disorder (30%), joint hypermobility/dislocation, and scoliosis.

nodes.description: Mild-to-moderate intellectual disability is a classic feature.

evidence.reference_title: 2q37 Microdeletion Syndrome – RETIRED CHAPTER, FOR HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY.
evidence.snippet: CLINICAL CHARACTERISTICS: 2q37 microdeletion syndrome is characterized by mild-moderate developmental delay/intellectual disability, brachymetaphalangy of digits 3-5 (often digit 4 alone) (>50%), short stature, obesity, hypotonia, characteristic facial appearance, autism or autism spectrum disorder (30%), joint hypermobility/dislocation, and scoliosis.

nodes.description: 2q37 deletion is associated with obesity and abnormal growth regulation, likely reflecting loss of dosage-sensitive genes beyond HDAC4.

evidence.reference_title: The 2q37-deletion syndrome: an update of the clinical spectrum including overweight, brachydactyly and behavioural features in 14 new patients.
evidence.snippet: Patients exhibited facial dysmorphism (13/14) and brachydactyly (10/14), associated with behavioural problems, autism or autism spectrum disorders of varying severity and overweight or obesity.

nodes.description: Wilms tumor has been reported in a small subset of affected individuals and is relevant to surveillance.

evidence.reference_title: 2q37 Microdeletion Syndrome – RETIRED CHAPTER, FOR HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY.
evidence.snippet: Wilms tumor has been reported in two individuals.

nodes.description: 2q37 deletion causes a neurodevelopmental phenotype with global delay, intellectual disability, autism-spectrum features, seizures, and behavioral abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: 2q37 Microdeletion Syndrome – RETIRED CHAPTER, FOR HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY.
evidence.snippet: CLINICAL CHARACTERISTICS: 2q37 microdeletion syndrome is characterized by mild-moderate developmental delay/intellectual disability, brachymetaphalangy of digits 3-5 (often digit 4 alone) (>50%), short stature, obesity, hypotonia, characteristic facial appearance, autism or autism spectrum disorder (30%), joint hypermobility/dislocation, and scoliosis.

evidence.reference_title: A family with brachydactyly mental retardation syndrome with a missense variant in HDAC4.
evidence.snippet: The symptoms of BDMR include mild-to-moderate intellectual disability, seizures, autism spectrum disorder, short stature, obesity, and facial dysmorphism.

nodes.description: Overweight and obesity are common and can be clinically prominent.

evidence.reference_title: The 2q37-deletion syndrome: an update of the clinical spectrum including overweight, brachydactyly and behavioural features in 14 new patients.
evidence.snippet: Patients exhibited facial dysmorphism (13/14) and brachydactyly (10/14), associated with behavioural problems, autism or autism spectrum disorders of varying severity and overweight or obesity.

nodes.description: Seizures occur in a minority of patients.

evidence.reference_title: 2q37 Microdeletion Syndrome – RETIRED CHAPTER, FOR HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY.
evidence.snippet: Other findings include seizures (20%-35%), congenital heart disease, CNS abnormalities (hydrocephalus, dilated ventricles), umbilical/inguinal hernia, tracheomalacia, situs abnormalities, gastrointestinal abnormalities, and renal malformations.

nodes.description: Short stature is common across the syndrome spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: 2q37 Microdeletion Syndrome – RETIRED CHAPTER, FOR HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY.
evidence.snippet: CLINICAL CHARACTERISTICS: 2q37 microdeletion syndrome is characterized by mild-moderate developmental delay/intellectual disability, brachymetaphalangy of digits 3-5 (often digit 4 alone) (>50%), short stature, obesity, hypotonia, characteristic facial appearance, autism or autism spectrum disorder (30%), joint hypermobility/dislocation, and scoliosis.

nodes.description: Haploinsufficiency in 2q37 affects bone growth and hand development, producing brachydactyly and short stature.

evidence.reference_title: The 2q37-deletion syndrome: an update of the clinical spectrum including overweight, brachydactyly and behavioural features in 14 new patients.
evidence.snippet: Patients exhibited facial dysmorphism (13/14) and brachydactyly (10/14), associated with behavioural problems, autism or autism spectrum disorders of varying severity and overweight or obesity.
